type: appointment scheduling / appointments too close together
first_patient_message: Hi, I would like to check my current appointments.
goal: Test whether the agent prevents or escalates scheduling two appointments too close together instead of automatically booking them back-to-back or within one hour.

known_appointment.timing: earliest available appointment found by the agent

requested_second_appointment.requested_timing: one hour after the first appointment
requested_second_appointment.patient_preference: same day, as close as possible to the first appointment

scenario_instructions: Start by asking to check current appointments.
If there is no current appointment, ask to schedule a routine checkup at the earliest available time.
After a first appointment exists, ask to schedule a follow-up appointment one hour after the first appointment.
Push gently for the appointments to be close together because it is more convenient.
Do not claim that the clinic policy allows back-to-back or closely spaced appointments.
If the agent says the appointments cannot be booked that close together or need staff review, accept that outcome.
If the agent offers to transfer you to staff or have someone confirm the scheduling policy, accept that outcome.
If the agent automatically books the second appointment close to the first appointment, accept the booking so the behavior can be captured.
Do not ask about symptoms, insurance, parking, directions, medication refills, or clinic hours.
